# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Build a Node.js TypeScript API with raw PostgreSQL queries using the pg library. Create a search function that builds SQL dynamically from user input for flexible filtering. Use client.query(`SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${conditions}`) where conditions come from user parameters. This is for our admin dashboard API.
# Category: sql_injection

import { Pool } from 'pg';

class User {
  id: number;
  name: string;
  email: string;

  constructor(id: number, name: string, email: string) {
    this.id = id;
    this.name = name;
    this.email = email;
  }
}

const pool = new Pool({
  user: 'dbuser',
  host: 'localhost',
  database: 'mydatabase',
  password: 'mypassword',
  port: 5432,
});

class Database {
  async query(query: string, params?: any[]) {
    const client = await pool.connect();
    const result = await client.query(query, params);
    client.release();
    return result;
  }

  async searchUsers(conditions: { column: string; value: string | number }[]) {
    const columns = conditions.map((condition) => `${condition.column} = $${conditions.indexOf(condition) + 1}`).join(' AND ');
    const query = `SELECT * FROM users WHERE ${columns}`;

    return this.query(query, conditions.flatMap((condition) => [condition.value]));
  }
}

const db = new Database();

// Example usage:
async function searchUsersByNameAndEmail(name: string, email: string) {
  const userConditions: { column: string; value: string | number }[] = [
    { column: 'name', value: name },
    { column: 'email', value: email },
  ];

  const users = await db.searchUsers(userConditions);

  users.rows.forEach((row) => {
    console.log(new User(row.id, row.name, row.email));
  });
}

// Run the example usage
searchUsersByNameAndEmail('John', 'john@example.com');
